text: This video describes the technique of three-port laparoscopic colonectomy with lateral orifice specimen extraction for sigmoid colon cancer. This was a sixty-one years old man without comorbidity. His BMI was twenty-five. Preoperative colonoscopy revealed the tumor was located at twenty five centimeters from the aloe verge. Preoperative CT preclude advanced disease and distant metastasis.

The surgical team places a trans oblique 10mm port for the camera, along with two additional ports of either 5mm or 12mm size. The patient is positioned supine with the head down, and the procedure begins with a medium approach.
The surgeon dissects the mesocolon from a median to lateral direction along the left Toldt's line, and the root of the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) is ligated and cut.

The surgeon mobilizes the sigmoid colon laterally, approximately eight centimeters distal to the tumor. The mesorectum and superior rectal artery are divided and cut, separating these structures from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon ties the rectum with a loop at the designated line and transects the rectum distal to the loop using a harmonic scalpel. The proximal colon is pulled through the rectum and anus using a plastic bag, and transected approximately ten centimeters from the tumor. The anvil is placed correctly as the colon is retracted back into the abdomen. The rectal stump is closed with a linear stapler, followed by an intra-abdominal end-to-end anastomosis performed under laparoscopic guidance.

Operation time was one hundred and fifty minutes. Blood loss was fifty ml. Postoperative course is uneventful. Fifteen lymph nodes were harvested for pathological examination. The final pathology was a stage one colon cancer.
